summary: High‑energy ’80 show with a smoking second set and standout Jerry solos, captured on a noisy but atmospheric audience mix.
review: The performance is praised for its relentless energy – the second set “smokes”, Brokedown Palace and Comes A Time receive glowing remarks, and Jerry’s solo is called an all‑time favorite. The recording is an audience (AUD) tape that many find gritty and occasionally muffled, yet it adds a live ambience that most reviewers enjoy despite its flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews (7 of 9) give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; recording quality is mixed but still listenable, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Noisy AUD tape with occasional muddiness and grating highs; some reviewers note the SBD version sounds sterile while the audience mix adds atmosphere.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is highlighted as a nonstop high‑energy stretch, though the transition from Truckin' into drums feels a bit rough; first set is solid with several tight jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his aggressive tone and an all‑time‑favorite solo; his playing is described as bright, far‑reaching, and heartfelt.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted as rich; a double‑time shuffle on drums highlighted Phil’s rhythmic drive, though some felt he didn’t step up during Brokedown Palace.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby's rhythm work praised, especially his backing behind Jerry’s solos and the quirky vocal moment when Garcia’s gear was pulled.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) receives praise for a solid pocket on Far From Me and overall good mesh with the band; keyboards are called pleasant.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mickey’s drumming gets a nod for double‑time shuffle, but the drum transition after Truckin' is cited as less smooth.
